widgets_values: Click Install Musubi-Tuner or Download Selected Models.

# VRGDG LTX LoRA Training Workflow

## What This Workflow Does

This workflow trains an LTX LoRA in chunks, creates preview videos after each saved chunk, and then builds a final XYZ comparison video when training reaches the final target step.

## Main Nodes

### `VRGDG_LTXLoraTrainChunk`
This is the main training node.

It handles:
- dataset folder prep
- optional caption creation
- cache creation and reuse
- musubi config generation
- LoRA training resume from latest state
- loading the newest trained LoRA back onto the `MODEL` output
- generating a safe `video_filename_prefix` for downstream preview videos

### `VRGDG_LTXPreviewXYZPlot`
This node creates a final comparison video from all saved preview videos.

It only runs when:
- `completed_steps >= total_target_steps`

It automatically:
- finds the preview videos for the current run
- figures out a square-ish grid layout
- labels each tile from the preview filename
- creates a new output filename if a prior XYZ video already exists

## Training Node Setup

### Dataset Folder
`dataset_images_dir` can be anywhere.

The node supports:
- pointing directly to an `images` folder
- or pointing to a parent dataset folder

If needed, it will organize images into an `images` subfolder.

Supported image types:
- `.png`
- `.jpg`
- `.jpeg`
- `.webp`
- `.bmp`
- `.gif`
- `.tif`
- `.tiff`

### Workspace Folder
`workspace_dir` can also be anywhere.

If it does not exist, the node creates it.

Inside that folder, the node creates:
- `cache`
- `output`
- `logs`
- `config`

## Important Training Behavior

### Resume
Each new run resumes from the latest saved state folder in:
- `workspace_dir/output`

### Save Behavior
`steps_per_run` is also the save interval.

That means:
- each run trains one chunk
- each run saves at the end of that chunk
- each run can immediately generate a preview video downstream

### Cache Reuse
If cache already exists and cache mode is set to auto:
- the node reuses cache
- it skips rebuilding cache

This is useful when retesting the workflow without waiting for cache again.

### Memory Cleanup
The node can clear Comfy memory before the Gemma text-encoder stage.

This helps reduce stalls during:
- text encoder caching
- heavy model loading

## Outputs Used Downstream

The training node outputs:
- `model`
- `latest_state_path`
- `log_path`
- `video_filename_prefix`
- `output_name`
- `completed_steps`
- `total_target_steps`

Most important downstream connections:
- `model` -> preview generation branch
- `video_filename_prefix` -> VHS `Combine Video` `filename_prefix`
- `output_name` -> `VRGDG_LTXPreviewXYZPlot`
- `completed_steps` -> `VRGDG_LTXPreviewXYZPlot`
- `total_target_steps` -> `VRGDG_LTXPreviewXYZPlot`

## Preview Video Naming

The training node outputs a VHS-safe prefix like:

`datasetname_YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM-SS/LTXChunkRun_step_250`

This means:
- all preview videos for one run go into the same dated folder
- each preview video includes the training step in its filename

That step number is what the XYZ node uses for tile labels.

## XYZ Plot Node Behavior

### When It Runs
It only creates the comparison video when training is finished:
- `completed_steps >= total_target_steps`

If not final yet:
- it does nothing

### Layout
The node now auto-calculates columns based on the number of preview videos.

Examples:
- `4 videos` -> `2 columns`
- `6 videos` -> `3 columns`
- `9 videos` -> `3 columns`

### Tile Size
If:
- `cell_width = 0`
- `cell_height = 0`

the node auto-detects preview resolution from the first video.

### Labels
If `label_tiles = true`:
- each tile gets a label above it
- the label is taken from the preview video filename

### Existing XYZ Outputs
If an XYZ compare video already exists:
- the node creates a new file with a suffix like `_v2`, `_v3`

It also ignores older `_XYZ_COMPARE_` files when gathering source previews.

## Render Backend

The XYZ node supports:
- `CPU (libx264)`
- `NVIDIA GPU (h264_nvenc)`

GPU mode speeds up encoding when NVENC is available.

## TensorBoard

The training config logs to TensorBoard.

You can use the `Show TensorBoard` button on the training node to open the training logs in a browser.

TensorBoard is useful for viewing:
- loss curves
- step progress
- training metrics over time

## Recommended Basic Flow

1. Set `dataset_images_dir`
2. Set `workspace_dir`
3. Set training values like `steps_per_run`, `total_target_steps`, `learning_rate`, and `blocks_to_swap`
4. Connect `model` output from trainer into your preview generation branch
5. Connect `video_filename_prefix` into VHS `Combine Video`
6. Connect `output_name`, `completed_steps`, and `total_target_steps` into `VRGDG_LTXPreviewXYZPlot`
7. Let the workflow loop until final step is reached
8. The XYZ node creates the final comparison video automatically at the end

nodes.title: Model link
widgets_values:
## Report workflow issue

If you found any issues when running this workflow, [report template issue here](https://github.com/Comfy-Org/workflow_templates/issues)


## Model links

**text_encoders**

- [qwen_3_4b.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_3_4b.safetensors)

**loras**

- [pixel_art_style_z_image_turbo.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/tarn59/pixel_art_style_lora_z_image_turbo/resolve/main/pixel_art_style_z_image_turbo.safetensors)

**diffusion_models**

- [z_image_turbo_bf16.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/diffusion_models/z_image_turbo_bf16.safetensors)

**vae**

- [ae.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo/resolve/main/split_files/vae/ae.safetensors)


Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 text_encoders/
│   │      └── qwen_3_4b.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 loras/
│   │      └── pixel_art_style_z_image_turbo.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 diffusion_models/
│   │      └── z_image_turbo_bf16.safetensors
│   └── 📂 vae/
│          └── ae.safetensors
```

Her makeup is polished with a soft glow. The setting is a luxurious urban rooftop overlooking a glowing city skyline. The color style is HDR with high contrast, creating a bold and glamorous mood. The camera angle is slightly low, capturing her in a powerful stance. Golden hour lighting with a warm sunset sky enhances the scene, with a gentle breeze moving her hair.

You can install it through comfyui which can be easier. Un mute the note to the right and use that.
